text.title: Tools, Services, Agent Workflow, and Gotchas
text.summary: CLI tools, platform services, recommended agent workflow, and AI failure modes for accessibility testing.

list.item: axe-core (via Playwright/Puppeteer) — automated WCAG scanning with JSON output; npm install axe-core
lighthouse CLI — Chrome-based a11y + performance audit; npm install -g lighthouse
pa11y — command-line a11y testing with multiple runner support; npm install -g pa11y
colour-contrast-checker — CLI contrast ratio verification; npm package
htmlcs — HTML_CodeSniffer for WCAG rule checking; squizlabs.github.io/HTML_CodeSniffer
Deque axe DevTools (SaaS) — AI-powered false-positive reduction, enterprise scanning, REST API + CLI; industry standard
Level Access (SaaS) — "Ask Level AI" chatbot for issue Q&A; enterprise VPAT generation; partial API
3Play Media (SaaS) — AI-enabled captions and audio descriptions; REST API; agent can submit video, poll for output
Siteimprove (SaaS) — continuous monitoring; good for regression detection in CI/CD; REST API
Accessibility Insights (OSS, Microsoft) — browser extension; excellent for manual testing; not agent-drivable

section.title: Agent Workflow

rule.statement: An agent can run axe-core via Playwright, parse the violation JSON, prioritize by WCAG criterion and user impact, and generate a developer-facing remediation brief with before/after code snippets. The agent must never mark issues as resolved — only humans or automated regression tests close findings.
rule.rationale: Agents provide scale and consistency in the audit-and-brief phase; human verification is required for the close phase because AT testing and accessibility judgment require human context.

section.reference: Playwright-based axe-core runner for agent use

antipattern.description: Agent runs axe-core without handling auth flows, cookie consent banners, or dynamic content loading
antipattern.reason: All three interfere with scan results. Agents must handle authentication, dismiss consent banners, and wait for dynamic content before running axe.

antipattern.description: Agent maps violations to incorrect WCAG criteria (e.g., citing 1.4.3 Contrast for a keyboard navigation issue)
antipattern.reason: LLMs confidently produce incorrect criterion references. Always validate criterion mappings against the WCAG 2.2 spec or a verified tool output before including in a report.

antipattern.description: Agent generates generic, keyword-stuffed alt text for complex charts and infographics
antipattern.reason: AI alt text defaults to describing the visual appearance, not the data or meaning. Prompt explicitly for functional, descriptive alt text that conveys the chart's insight.

antipattern.description: Agent scans only the landing page of an SPA, missing component-level violations on navigated routes
antipattern.reason: SPA frameworks rebuild the DOM on navigation. Agents must trigger navigation events and re-run axe on each route.

antipattern.description: Agent marks an ARIA-dependent issue resolved without AT verification
antipattern.reason: Assistive technology behavior (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver) diverges significantly from automated tool predictions. AT-dependent issues require AT verification before closing.
